title: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol
description: Automatically assigns IP addresses and network configuration to devices.
fullExplanation: DHCP automates network configuration. When a device connects to a network, it broadcasts a DHCP Discover message. A DHCP server responds with an Offer (available IP address). The client Requests that IP, and the server Acknowledges (ACK). This is called the DORA process (Discover, Offer, Request, Acknowledge). DHCP also provides subnet mask, default gateway, DNS servers, and lease time.

steps.name: DHCP Discover
steps.description: Client broadcasts 'I need an IP address' (source 0.0.0.0, dest 255.255.255.255)

steps.name: DHCP Offer
steps.description: Server offers an available IP address and configuration

steps.name: DHCP Request
steps.description: Client requests the offered IP (broadcasts to inform other servers)

steps.name: DHCP Acknowledge
steps.description: Server confirms IP assignment, provides lease time

providedInfo: Lease Time (how long IP is valid)
keyFacts: DORA process: Discover, Offer, Request, Acknowledge
Lease time typically 24 hours to 7 days
Client must renew lease before expiration
DHCP Relay forwards DHCP across subnets
Static IP reservations based on MAC address
DHCP Snooping prevents rogue DHCP servers

leaseProcess.action: Client tries to renew with same server
If renewal failed, client broadcasts renewal request
Lease expires, client must start DORA again
